title: これ、スタックしている？ • 現在タスクは thin-core を維持したまま drift 耐性と自動反 映を次段まで進めること として扱います。まず現行の lane 可 用性を current-run で確認し、6 本の active lane を立てて から実装に入ります。 • Spawned Feynman [reviewer] └ [phase:plan] [family:layering] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: continue Kernel hardening without increasing always-loaded con...
summary_text: **Findings** - High: `blocked` semantics are dropped when leaving local scripts. `run-linked-systems.sh` computes `blocked_count` internally but dispatches only `success_count` and `error_count`, so consumer repos cannot distinguish retryable blocked states from hard errors in metrics/comments. [run-linked-systems.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-linked-systems.sh#L247) [run-linked-systems.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-linked-systems.sh#L304) [fugue-result-receiver.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/.github/workflows/templates/fugue-result-receiver.yml#L27) - High: CI gate does not enforce the new regressions for linked exit contract and backup lock behavior. `tests/test-linked-systems-exit-contract.sh` and `tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh` are only wired in the local hook, not in GitHub Actions gate jobs. This allows regressions to merge without signal. [fugue-harness-hook.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/harness/fugue-harness-hook.sh#L113) [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml#L254) [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml#L223) - Medium: launchd overlap now emits recurring tempfail (`75`) every interval when scan lock is held. This is contract-correct, but with `StartInterval=30` it can generate repeated failure-like logs/alerts under long scans, which is an operational compatibility risk if supervisors treat non-zero as incident. [bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh#L10) [bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh#L83) [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh#L209) - Medium: fail-closed propagation now reaches `gha24` fallback caller unchanged, so `blocked` from linked systems (`75`) aborts the full local fallback path. If linked systems were intended as best-effort in fallback mode, this is a behavior break. [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1075) [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1374) [gha24](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/gha24#L559) **Open Questions / Assumptions** - Should `blocked` in linked systems be treated as retryable warning (continue orchestration) or hard stop in all callers, including `gha24` fallback? - Should consumer dispatch contract include `blocked_count` (and optionally `blocked_reason`) as required fields? **Summary** - Core local fail-closed behavior is implemented correctly, but cross-boundary propagation and CI enforcement are still incomplete. - The biggest remaining risk is silent regression in CI plus semantic loss (`blocked` flattening) outside local execution.
